message:
fix(distributed): handle IPv6 and raw IP addresses in TLS server name parsing

build_tls_server_name previously naively split on ':' to extract the host
from a bind address, which broke on IPv6 addresses like [::1]:4141 and
failed to produce ServerName::IpAddress for numeric hosts. This caused
TLS/SNI verification failures when binding to IP addresses rather than
hostnames.

Extracted host-parsing logic into distributed_bind_host() which properly
handles:
- Full SocketAddr parsing (covers both v4 and v6 with port)
- Bracketed IPv6 notation ([::1]:port)
- Simple host:port with single-colon heuristic
- Bare hostnames/IPs without port

IP addresses now produce ServerName::IpAddress instead of attempting DNS
name conversion, which would always fail for numeric strings.

Added three targeted tests covering IPv6 bind addr, IPv4 bind addr, and
hostname bind addr to prevent regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
